en.extract:
The Turkestan short-toed lark (Alaudala heinei) is a species of lark in the family Alaudidae. It is found in Ukraine and central Turkey through parts of Central Asia and southern Siberia west to south-central Mongolia and south to southern Afghanistan. This species and the Mediterranean short-toed lark (A. rufescens) were formerly considered conspecific and called the lesser short-toed lark, but a 2020 study recovered them as distinct species.


Etymology
The genus name Alaudala is the diminutive of Alauda. The specific name heinei commemorates the German ornithologist Ferdinand Heine.


Subspecies
Four subspecies are recognized:

Alaudala heinei pseudobaetica - (Stegmann, 1932): Found in eastern Turkey, Transcaucasia and northern Iran
Alaudala heinei heinei - (Homeyer, 1873): Found from Ukraine to eastern Kazakhstan
Alaudala heinei aharonii - (Hartert, 1910): Found in central Turkey
Alaudala heinei persica - Sharpe, 1890: Found in eastern and southern Iraq to southern and south-western Afghanistan


References

International Ornithological Congress (January 23, 2021). "IOC World Bird List". Retrieved January 23, 2021.

The '''Turkestan short-toed lark''' ('''''Alaudala heinei''''') is a species of [[lark]] in the family [[Alaudidae]]. It is found in [[Ukraine]] and central [[Turkey]] through parts of [[Central Asia]] and southern [[Siberia]] west to south-central [[Mongolia]] and south to southern [[Afghanistan]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/larks/|title=Nicators, reedling & larks « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|access-date=2021-01-19}}</ref> This species and the [[Mediterranean short-toed lark]] (''A. rufescens'') were formerly considered conspecific and called the [[lesser short-toed lark]], but a 2020 study recovered them as distinct species.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Alaudala [rufescens or heini, excl. niethammeri] (Mediterranean or Turkestan Short-toed Lark [excl. niethammeri]) - Avibase|url=https://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=F8736B10|access-date=2021-01-30|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|date=2021-01-01|title=Multiple species delimitation approaches applied to the avian lark genus Alaudala|journal=Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution|language=en|volume=154|article-number=106994|doi=10.1016/j.ympev.2020.106994|issn=1055-7903|doi-access=free|last1=Alström |first1=Per |last2=Van Linschooten |first2=Jip |last3=Donald |first3=Paul F. |last4=Sundev |first4=Gombobaatar |last5=Mohammadi |first5=Zeinolabedin |last6=Ghorbani |first6=Fatemeh |last7=Shafaeipour |first7=Arya |last8=Van Den Berg |first8=Arnoud |last9=Robb |first9=Magnus |last10=Aliabadian |first10=Mansour |last11=Wei |first11=Chentao |last12=Lei |first12=Fumin |last13=Oxelman |first13=Bengt |last14=Olsson |first14=Urban |pmid=33250446 }}</ref>

== Etymology ==
The genus name ''Alaudala'' is the [[diminutive]] of ''[[Alauda]].'' The specific name ''heinei'' commemorates the German ornithologist [[Ferdinand Heine]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Jobling |first=James A. |title=The Helm Dictionary of Scientific Bird Names: From Aalge to Zusii |date=2010 |publisher=Christopher Helm |isbn=978-1-4081-2501-4 |edition=1. Aufl. |location=London}}</ref>

==Subspecies==
Four subspecies are recognized:<ref name=":0" />

* ''Alaudala heinei pseudobaetica'' - <small>([[Boris K. Stegmann|Stegmann]], 1932)</small>: Found in eastern Turkey, [[Transcaucasia]] and northern Iran
* ''Alaudala heinei heinei'' - <small>([[Eugen Ferdinand von Homeyer|Homeyer]], 1873)</small>: Found from Ukraine to eastern Kazakhstan
* ''Alaudala heinei aharonii'' - <small>(Hartert, 1910)</small>: Found in central Turkey
* ''Alaudala heinei persica'' - <small>[[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1890</small>: Found in eastern and southern Iraq to southern and south-western Afghanistan

zh.extract:
中亚短趾百灵（学名：Alaudala heinei）是雀形目百灵科的一种鸟类。


特征
中亚短趾百灵体重约24.1克，翼长约86.1毫米，嘴峰长约10.5毫米，喙宽度约4.2毫米，喙厚度约4.8毫米，跗蹠长约18.2毫米，尾长约54毫米。中亚短趾百灵是部分迁徙的鸟类，其栖息在开放的草原环境中，食性为杂食性。


亚种
A. h. heinei：繁殖区从乌克兰和土耳其东部至哈萨克斯坦东部和蒙古南部；在中东和亚洲西南部越冬。
A. h. aharonii：繁殖区在土耳其中部（安纳托利亚高原）；部分迁徙，在叙利亚中部越冬。
A. h. persica：分布于伊拉克南部和伊朗至阿富汗东南部。
